title: Chorizo al Vino Tinto
subtitle: Spanish chorizo cooked in red wine
excerpt: Chorizo al vino tinto is sliced chorizo cooked in red wine until the wine reduces to a sticky glaze around the sausage. It takes ten minutes and is one of the simplest Spanish tapas. Serve with bread to mop the pan juices.
sourceNotes: Chorizo cooked in wine is a common preparation across Spain, appearing as a tapa in bars and as a simple supper dish at home. The wine reduces into a rich, savoury sauce around the chorizo.

content.text: Chorizo cooked in red wine is a quick Spanish tapa of sliced cured sausage simmered in wine until the liquid reduces to a thick, glossy sauce. The chorizo fat renders into the wine and makes a rich cooking liquid that is as good as the sausage itself. Serve with bread to mop the pan.

items.prepNote: cooking chorizo or cured, sliced 1 cm thick

content.text: Place the sliced chorizo in a cold frying pan. Set over medium heat. The chorizo will begin to render its fat as it warms. Cook for 2 minutes each side until lightly browned at the edges.
Add the sliced garlic and bay leaves if using. Cook for 30 seconds.

Pour in {{red-wine}} of red wine. Let it bubble and reduce over medium-high heat for about 8 minutes until the wine has reduced to a thick, dark glaze that coats the chorizo. Remove the bay leaves. Scatter with parsley and serve immediately with bread.

items.symptom: The sauce reduces to a burnt sticky residue
items.cause: Heat too high or reduced for too long
items.fix: Watch the pan from about 5 minutes in. The sauce should be thick and glossy but still fluid. Add a splash of water and stir if it's going too fast.

content.text: Chorizo al vino tinto is a bar staple and a home cook's go-to tapa when guests arrive unexpectedly. It takes ten minutes and requires almost nothing in the way of technique. A Spanish red is the right choice for the wine, though any dry red you'd drink will do.
